rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical oxyhalocyclization of 2-vinyl anilides with nBu4NX to give 4-halomethyl 4H-3,1-benzoxazines using 4CzIPN as photocatalyst under 455 nm irradiation with Pt electrodes.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of halide anion (Cl-) to molecular halogen (Cl2); the photoexcited 4CzIPN* further activates Cl2 (via oxidative quenching or energy transfer) to generate chlorine radicals, while the cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: molecular chlorine (Cl2) generated by anodic oxidation of Cl-
chloronium/onium cation from Cl2 addition to vinyl anilide (Int-I)
oxidized intermediate Int-III prior to deprotonation/aromatization
chlorine radical (Cl•) from photoactivation of Cl2
benzyl cation formed by oxidation of benzyl radical (alternative path)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Cl- to Cl2; in path A Cl2 adds to the alkene as an onium cation that is intramolecularly trapped by the amide oxygen to deliver the benzoxazine; in path B 4CzIPN* (via oxidative quenching or EnT) homolyzes Cl2 to Cl•, which adds to the alkene to give a benzyl radical that is further oxidized to a benzyl cation prior to cyclization. Protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield decreased (to 61/31/33/30% with 2.0–5.0 equiv BHT) but reaction not fully suppressed, indicating involvement of a radical pathway alongside a non-radical (ionic) pathway.

notes: Two parallel mechanisms are proposed: (favored/primary) ionic path through Cl2 addition giving an onium cation that is cyclized by the carbonyl oxygen; (alternative) photoredox/EnT radical path via Cl• addition and oxidation of the benzyl radical to a cation. BHT does not fully inhibit the reaction, consistent with both ionic and radical contributions. Gram-scale and late-stage (dehydrocholic acid-derived 1r) reactions validate practicality but do not provide mechanistic data.
